description: Letter by the Jewish community of the village of Qalhā, Egypt, apparently to Eli ha-Kohen b. Yahya in Fustat, 2nd half of the 11th century. The Jews of Qalhā report to the Parnas in Fustat that Abū ʿImrān Mūsa b. Yequtiel arrived in their village on 17 Tammuz, injured and/or sick from the violence he experienced during the nahb (plunder) of Fayyūm. In addition he was robbed of everything by robbers near the village. He died a few weeks later, in Av. One of his two wives and the daughter of the other died also. The remaining wife was brought to Fustat at the expense of the community, because she had a cousin (ibn ʿamm) there. The little boy could not live in the village because of the Kushim (Sudanese soldiers?). Information from Goitein's note card.
tags: unrest, war, polygyny, illness letter 969-1517, Qalha, communal, 11th c, Eli b. Yahya, shidda, Fayyum, Kushim, Illness
description_he: מכתב מקהילת כפר קלהא, מצרים, לעלי הכהן בן יחיא בפוסטאט, מחצית שנייה של המאה ה-11. על אבו עמראן מוסא בן יקותיאל שהגיע פצוע ממאורעות שד פיום, נשדד, ונפטר לאחר שבועות. שתי נשותיו/בנותיו מתו גם הן.
